reviews.review_msg: Great at bringing material across

I'm surprised that Lawrence Boland is still teaching.  I attended his Econ 305 course back in 1986.  Changed my life.  He was engaging and spent the time to challenge the students if you were up to the challenge.  Never looked back.  I should drop in an visit him if he is still around.
One of the best professors I ever had.

He is my top 3 professor in economic department. I used to think 3 hours class would be really boring, but, not at all, his class is really interest. Also, 3hours class had been passed really fast. However, asking questions that you donot understand marterial Between, He is a nicest and most intelligence professor at the Economic deparment.

Great! This is a class where I actually learned something that stuck with me and is  interesting. It's also refreshing to see an econ prof who is critical of unrealistic econ models and is not credulous enough to take them up on faith, unlike other simpleminded self deluding economists. Boland - one of the top three profs I ever had!
great class! we had interesting discussions about the great economic thinkers, and the social contexts which influenced their ideas. if you're interested in economics, this class is a must!

Great prof! keeps things interesting, class is formatted so you do your readings, then ask questions about them. All you need to do is ask questions and you will do well! Class is mostly based on class participation. Very interesting course

Probably one of the best profs I've had at SFU. Talks about current events and relates it to the reading. He doesn't encourage you to ask about ur grades but he does encourage you to ask questions! I enjoyed it very much. He seems like a mean guy but it's all an act he's in fact a very nice guy!
Best class I've ever attended. Boland is very knowlidgable and wise. I would love it if all my classes were run this way.

This professor is probably the best professor I have had so far in school. His teaching method requires participation and is unique, but if you're able to participate and listen, you will enjoy him. supposedly econ110 is no longer being taught, but any class with him is a chance to learn something.

If you have some extra cash laying around and you dont want to learn anything, then take his class. Basically a waste of tuition fees. Class is a joke, marking system is a joke, prof is a joke. Its like a comedy act but the joke is on you at the end. I was one of the students that did well on the midterms, participated fully and ended up with a B.
The best prof ever!!!!!!
Best prof I have ever had. Ever.
No Comments
No Comments
great prof. very unconventional class in a good way. a refreshing reminder of what class should really be like.
No Comments

Mr. boland is a very knowledgable prof, although he seems to be very ignorant to things that he beleives are wrong. not very open minded. he seems to think that his is the only class in the world, and that students have all the time in the world for his antics. take his class if you don't mind lisening to a sour old man 3 times a week...enjoy!
ask questions, ask ask ask.. participation in class is a huge portion of your final grade. very smart guy!

SFU needs more professors like Lawrence Boland! Challenges you to think, rather than memorize. As long as you are willing to participate, and actually do the readings, you are guaranteed a good grade....participation in class is IMPORTANT...make sure to ask  a lot of questions!

Had him for Econ110 2 sem. ago. Interesting style. Class is small 20-30 ppl. *NOTE you MUST ask questions (participate) or you will fail! Most ppl drop this class becuz of this reason. And need to read short books. The first midterm was on the books we read, and most ppl didnot read them so 1/2 of the class failed(me too) but the final fair.

The Prof expects you to ask questions and actively participate in discussion in EVERY lecture. If your 1st language is not English, you are a DEAD DUCK. Midterm passing mark is 60%. Over 50% students failed.
The best class ever! Just attend and participate and you get your A!
very interesting prof...fun lecture!!
Awesome prof. Very good course. Unique teaching style that is quite nice for change. Encourages learning rather then memorizing a textbook.
Very interesting professor. Makes you think, which is what a teacher should do.
Awesome prof. Rare and unique teaching style that actually ends up with you learning something. Recommended++
he is very intresting

A true academic- either love him or hate him. I think his teaching style is great. Every Arts undergrad should take his class... tests your intellectual worth, but don't take him too seriously... have fun
Awesome class and very easy
A very unique prof compared to Econ faculty.Easy course if you do what he says.Participate!Nice guy to talk to.

For sure, he is not an ordinary prof. However, as long as you do the reading and pay attention to the discussion in the class, you will find it very easy to get an A. I am very glad that I took this course since I learned so much from him. Try not to mind
Easily the best prof at SFU.

Very arrogant. He thinks that what he is doing in class is the most important thing in the world. He tends to judge people before he knows them and he'll also make fun of, and belittle students in class. However, the class was fun and easy.
Easy course.  Read material and participate in class.
REALLY GOOD, read the material, do the assignment, ask questions!!! EASY A
nice guy, just pay attention in class, read all the books and u should be fine. discuss interesting topics in lectures as well
Very enjoyable class. Pay attention, write notes and participate and you'll really enjoy this class. Great prof.
Pointless class and his teaching material was totally irrelevant in regards to the course...  Has very political views and is very biased in his marking
best class ever. just don't let him know your political beliefs. he will give you a hard time no matter what they are.  you are not allowed to take notes in his class.
First time I ever learned something was in this class
best one at SFU
Pay attention in class and u'll ace his course
No Comments
Good prof. lectures are based on your questions. Just don't **** the guy off.
